this diagram models a system with multiple components interacting through apis and associations.
the main flows involve configuration settings, notification handling, user interactions with services and needs, and data exchange through api_manager.
key edge cases include data transfer between components and user interactions with services and needs.

this diagram models a login process involving a main process, a localuser, and an api manager.
the main process retrieves a token and uses it to request data from the api manager.
if the token is valid and the status is ok, the process continues normally.
if the token is invalid or the status is not ok, the localuser requests user data.
the localuser then creates a new instance and the api manager completes the action.
the process involves checking token validity and status, requesting user data, and handling responses in json format.
main -> api_manager — type=message; label="listjson=request()"; explanation="request json list"
api_manager -> service/need/report — type=message; label="[for each entry in listjson]: new"; explanation="create new service/need/report for each entry"
service/need/report -> main — type=message; label="displaylist()"; explanation="display list of services/needs/reports"
flow 1: main -> api_manager -> service/need/report -> main
this sequence diagram models the process of reading and displaying a list of services/needs/reports.
the application starts with the main participant, which sends a request to the api_manager to get a json list.
the api_manager processes the request and creates a new service/need/report for each entry in the json list.
the service/need/report participant then sends back a display list to the main participant, which displays the list of services/needs/reports.
the process repeats for each entry in the json list, creating multiple instances of service/need/report.

this sequence diagram models the insertion process involving a client (main) interacting with a server (api_manager) and a service (service/need/report). the client initiates the process by sending a "new" message to the server, which then converts the data into json format and sends it to the service for processing. upon completion, the service returns a "done" message back to the server, which then communicates the status to the client.

this sequence diagram models the interaction between the main application, api manager, and service/need/report.
the main application prints data to the screen, retrieves updated data, processes each item, converts it to json format, and requests the update entry.
the api manager handles the retrieval, processing, and conversion of data, and signals completion.
there is a loop in the main application for processing each updated data item.
the diagram highlights the flow of data and control between the components, ensuring the update process is completed successfully.
